frameworks.name: 日夜節律與光曝露
frameworks.desc: L01 Light Exposure 與 L03 Circadian Lighting Design 對應 circadian photoentrainment、daylight、electric light 與 Equivalent Melanopic Lux，目標是降低 circadian phase disruption。

frameworks.name: 視覺照明與工作面需求
frameworks.desc: L02 Visual Lighting Design 要依 space types、activities 與 work planes 提供適當 illuminance，支援 visual comfort 與 visual acuity。

frameworks.name: 眩光、平衡與電光品質
frameworks.desc: L04 Electric Light Glare Control、L07 Visual Balance 與 L08 Electric Light Quality 關注 glare、visual balance、color rendering 與 flicker。

frameworks.name: 日光設計與模擬決策
frameworks.desc: L05 Daylight Design Strategies、L06 Daylight Simulation 與 L09 Occupant Lighting Control 要把 daylight access、simulation reports、outdoor views 與 occupant preferences 納入設計判斷。

questions.stem: What sort of fixtures would have a UGR?
Fluctuating light levels impact which of the following?
According to Feature L08: Electric Light Quality, light fixtures with higher color rendering do what?

options.text: Emit light that is brighter and more cost efficient.
Emit light that gives off less heat.
Emit light that show colors unrealistically.
Emit light that show colors realistically.

questions.source_explanation:
Identifying and utilizing lighting fixtures that emit a high quality of light and do not display signs of 
flicker contributes to a comfortable and healthy space. Light fixtures with higher color rendering emit 
light that show colors realistically. Color Rendering Index (CRI) and IES TM-30-15 are commonly 
used metrics used to determine the color rendering properties of a light source.

questions.stem: A project team working on a university research building is preparing documentation for WELL Certification. What should they include to demonstrate compliance with Feature L06: Daylight Simulation?
Age-related visual issues are caused all of the following except:

options.text: Increased light absorption by the lenses

questions.stem: In Feature L07: Visual Balance, lighting is designed by a lighting professional to take into account the following considerations except?

options.text: Illuminance uniformity on horizontal task planes

questions.stem: What documentation should be submitted for verification of L04: Electric Light Glare Control?
Points in which feature fulfill the requirements of L01: Light Exposure?
A project team is pursuing Feature LO5: Daylight Design Strategies. To obtain the maximum points for Part 2: Integrated Solar Shading, windows should?

options.text: Have automated shading to prevent glare.
Have shading that can be opened every day.
Be smaller than larger than 0. 55 m2 (6 ft2) to minimize light.

questions.stem: If a team wants to earn 2 points in Feature L04: Electric Light Glare Control for their new industrial manufacturing center, they must choose fixtures with what maximum UGR?
How far away should supplemental lighting be installed from the front edge of a workstation in Part 2: Provide Supplemental Lighting of Feature L09: Occupant Lighting Control?
Which feature lists acceptable lighting reference guidelines?
Which of the following is NOT one of the elements of lighting that occupants may control under Part 1: Enhance Occupant Controllability of Feature L09: Occupant Lighting Control?

options.text: Distribution of light by controlling different groups of lights or through preset scenes.

questions.stem: The impact of light on the circadian system can be measured using a metric called?
Which of the following features sets the minimum distances from windows to regularly occupied spaces?
questions.source_explanation:
To ensure that daylight is maximized within buildings, this feature sets minimum 
distances from windows to regularly occupied spaces. - WELL Building Standard, 
Feature L01

questions.stem: Which of the following terms refers to windows and/or glazing in the outdoor envelope of a building?
Which of the following features outlines design parameters for windows to optimize the quantity and quality of daylight while minimizing unwanted glare?

options.text: Feature L04: Electric Light Glare Control

questions.stem: A new corporate headquarters building is being constructed in a dense urban area. During the design phase, lighting simulations demonstrate that 40% of regularly occupied space receives at least 300 lux (28fc) for over 50% of operating hours each year. What should the designers change to earn 2 points in Feature L01: Light Exposure?

options.text: Increase window area to ensure that 50% of regularly occupied space is reached by natural light.
Introduce light shelves to increase minimum lux received to 400 lux.
Include light wells to ensure that 75% of regularly occupied space is reached by natural light.
Change building orientation to ensure that natural light reaches regularly occupied space for more than 60% of operating hours each year.

questions.source_explanation:
For Feature L06 Daylight Simulation, 55% of the space must receive at least 300 lux 
(28fc) of sunlight for at least 50% of operating hours each year for 1 point.

questions.stem: DALY is an acronym for what?

Many features in the WELL Building Standard require performance testing to verify the feature. For Part 1: Provide Visual Acuity of Feature L02: Visual Lighting Design, a Visual Lighting performance test is required. What are the 5 parts of performance tests for feature verification?

options.text: Test Locations & Conditions, Test Method, Test Quantity, Reporting & Compliance, Device Requirements.
Test Parameters, Test Quality, Reporting Metrics, Device Options.
Reporting, Physical Samples, Photographic Evidence, Compliance, Conditions.

questions.stem: Humans are diurnal, meaning that we are prone to wakefulness during the day and sleepiness at night. What system in our bodies does light exposure stimulate?
Electric lighting used indoors has a low frequency of flicker that are not present in daylight. What health conditions has Flicker been associated with?
What is an AAP?
Projects may use spreadsheets published by IWBI to calculate what?
What is the intent of Feature L09: Occupant Lighting Control?

options.text: Maximize personal responsibility in regards to electricity.
Provide individuals with access to customizable lighting environments.
To allow for personal style in individual workspaces.
